question: Does grounding word meaning in explicit OKF definitions (A1) and composing meaning with a symbolic concept grammar (A2) beat an ungrounded distributional baseline (A0) on definition-faithfulness (D1) and compositional derivation (D2)?

arms.A0: base model only, CLOSED-BOOK (D1: define from parametric knowledge, no glosses shown; D2: answer directly)
arms.A1: base + retrieved OKF definition (genuine grounding; D2: no-op, nothing to retrieve)
arms.A2: base + grounding + reasoning scaffold (D1: provenance constraint; D2: chain-of-thought derivation — the engine verdict is NEVER injected)

primaryMetric: paired delta per metric vs A0 (A1-A0 and A2-A0), counting CI-clean improvements
primaryN: D1=34 cases (24 train / 10 eval), D2=41 cases over 10 worlds (23 train / 18 eval), x >=3 seeds (Phase 1 must scale N before any claim)
folds: every case carries a deterministic train/eval fold (build_dataset.fold_of, ~70/30); Phase-2 training (tools/wiki_to_sense_training.py) uses the train fold only, the Phase-2 uplift is measured on the eval fold (run_semantic_grounding_eval.py --fold eval); folds are disjoint by construction

taskSuccess.rule: answer-rate / fluency on D1+D2 does not regress vs A0 (CI-clear of 0)

datasetValidity.rule: D2_dataset_valid == true (committed gold matches the Datalog derivation)

protected.rule: no protected_history/religion identity claims enter the D2 axiom seed; cross-tradition stays abstain

stoppingRule: anytime-valid confidence sequence (tools/eval_stats.confidence_sequence_mean) REQUIRED for any peeked metric
decontam: tools/assert_semantic_grounding_decontam.py — independent exact/normalized + content-shingle (Jaccard) disjointness of eval prompts vs committed training packs
ranking: guardrails first, then count + magnitude of CI-clean A1/A2 improvements over A0
claimCeiling: candidate_only; canClaimAGI:false; narrow seed-bound feasibility; a faithfulness/composition result, NOT understanding
honestLimits: small N (D1=34, D2=41; eval fold smaller still) — underpowered for a 10pt effect; report mde_at_n and treat single-run deltas as coarse

D1 closed-book SENSE scoring is LOW-VALIDITY: it lexically matches the model's free-form definition to the OKF glosses, which are provenance-focused (not genus-differentia), so a valid definition can be mis-scored for not using OKF's framing. The robust D1 axis is FAITHFULNESS (does the model assert a forbidden author?), which is a clean closed-book test. An LLM-judge sense grader is a future upgrade.
D2 is a closed-world grounding verdict over hand-written axioms, not a truth oracle

A2-D2 is now a real chain-of-thought arm (the model derives the verdict); the engine verdict is NO LONGER injected, so the earlier tautology is removed. The load-bearing comparisons are A0 vs A1 (does retrieved grounding help) and A0 vs A2 (does the reasoning/provenance scaffold help)

enforcement.claim_gate: tools/claim_gate.py --prefix semantic-grounding --spec <this file> (power, uncertainty, construct-family, decontam, magnitude)
